papers.title_en: Magneto-rotational instabilities in solids: application to neutron-star crusts
papers.authors: Arthur G. Suvorov, Thomas Celora, Kostas D. Kokkotas
papers.evidence: 研究中子星壳层中的磁旋转不稳定性，与内部动力学和脉冲星故障相关
papers.tldr: 磁旋转不稳定性（MRI）在固体中的可行性研究，以中子星壳层为背景。通过简化平面平行分析，发现仅当剪切流极强、磁张力超过固体剪切模量时，MRI才能增长。对双中子星并合而言，这意味着壳层磁场放大要求自旋频率≥300 Hz；若粘性加热削弱晶格，所需频率将降低。该工作揭示了弹性对MRI的抑制条件，并为解释中子星并合前磁场增强提供了新机制。
papers.abstract_en: The magneto-rotational instability can generate strong, turbulent substructure within magnetised shear flows. The efficacy of the mechanism as a function of microphysical aspects of the fluid, such as stratification and diffusivity, has been explored extensively. One aspect that has not been studied thus far, however, is whether the instability can also operate in solids. Motivated by the possibility that solid regions within planets or degenerate stars may rotate differentially with respect to liquid or gaseous layers during some phase of their life, we examine the extent to which elasticity suppresses the instability. A simplified, plane-parallel analysis reveals that only in cases where the flow is strongly sheared, such that the magnetic tension that would result from the instability in a liquid exceeds the shear modulus of the elastic cavity, can magnetic growth occur. In the context of dynamical tides in binary neutron-star mergers, this implies that the magnetic field can be amplified in the crust prior to coalescence only if the star boasts a spin frequency of $\gtrsim 300$Hz. If viscous heating weakens the crystalline structure prior to resonance, the required spin frequency is reduced.
